metadata.description: Major pre-submission pass implementing user's 6 items: switch headline to 94.9%/2, drop keyword-probe approach, drop numbered hypotheses, soften four-mechanisms framing, execute audit fix list, run UK-English verification agent. Verify before + verify after.

summary:
Completed all 6 user-directed revisions plus audit-list fixes. Headline now 94.9%/2-topics. Keyword-probe approach removed entirely. Numbered hypotheses dropped throughout body, captions, and SI. Four-mechanisms framing softened while preserving the distinct phenomena. UK English verified by dedicated subagent and 13 additional fixes applied. Abstract 249 words. 191/191 tests pass. 8 consistency checks all clean.

WHAT IS NEXT:
1. User should review the reframed abstract + H1 Results section for the 94.9%/2 + 99.9%/4 dual framing to confirm tone is right.
2. MLP-description expansion in Methods is the minimum-safe version (architecture + R²); if you have the exact loss/optimiser/split information, that could be added for full reproducibility.
3. The 5 citation-fit concerns from the prior audit (Sichach+Pane, Jiang+Kawakami-Venkatagiri as foil, Entman-for-second-level-agenda-setting, Baeyaert 2025 whose DOI was 403-blocked, Matsui & Ferrara) remain flagged for manual review.
4. NMS2026 parallel version still un-synced with all session changes.
5. Topic labels (Artist Defense Tools, Decentralized Infrastructure, etc.) kept in US spelling because they are pipeline data-file strings — changing them in manuscript would create data/manuscript divergence.
6. Deadline April 30, 2026 (16 days).

contribution.description: Executed user's 6-item revision plan: (1) switched headline metric to 94.9%/2-topics as the striking finding, with 99.9%/4 retained only where the tail enumeration is needed; (2) dropped keyword-based probe approach entirely from Methods, Results, and SI, leaving embedding-based 750-probe set as canonical; (3) clarified that four mechanisms sit inside what was the third angle (not a competing count), softened the explicit "four mechanisms" drumbeat, removed the "### Four Mechanisms of Compression" Discussion section header in favour of "### Mechanisms of Compression"; (4) dropped the numbered hypotheses throughout — deleted Intro §Hypotheses subsection, removed (H1)/(H2)/(H3) from Results subsection headers, rewrote Results Summary as prose without H-labels, removed all 4 remaining H-refs in body/captions/SI; (5) ran UK English verification subagent and applied 13 additional UK fixes (democratisation, liberalising, platformisation, optimisation, personalisation, normalised, visualisation); (6) elaborated quadruple parallelism for the user with examples and alternatives in the conversation. Additionally executed audit fix list from prior session: MLP training expansion, k=20 criteria in-text, Robustness Checks stat naming, Latour 1992 misapplication replaced and removed from bibliography, Bowker & Star 1999 specified as residual-categories concept, Post-Mediatized Sphere subsection compressed to one paragraph, grammar fix at L325, 5 US-spelling slips (personalization, unauthorized ×2, editorialized ×2). 191/191 tests pass. All 3 .docx files regenerated. Abstract 249 words (under 250 limit). Verification: 8 consistency checks clean across all 3 files (H-labels 0, 906 refs 0, keyword-based 0, four mechanisms 0, em-dashes 0, Latour 0, 99.9%-in-4-topics headline 0, US-spelling body 0).

context.conversation_snippet: "carefully implement the necessary changes for each of these issues... (1) use best metrics 94.9%/2 (2) drop keyword approach everywhere (3) resolve mechanisms/hypotheses (4) drop numbered hypotheses (5) UK English check agent (6) elaborate quadruple parallelism"
